summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on regulation, product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: PayPal takes Venmo global
articles.description: The peer-to-peer payment app will be available to users in 90 countries by integrating with PayPal accounts, the company said Monday.

articles.title: Synchrony, others broaden horizons to tap top talent
articles.description: Payments companies are often seeking experience outside of financial services, including in tech, in their search for new hires.

articles.title: NY forces cash acceptance
articles.description: New York Attorney General Letitia James reminded consumers and businesses that a new state law, effective Saturday, makes it illegal to decline cash payments.

articles.title: Visa, PayPal execs react to K-shaped economy
articles.description: The economic model suggests some consumers are becoming wealthier, while the economic well-being of others descends. Here’s how some industry executives view the impact.

articles.title: Stablecoin use surges
articles.description: The total transaction volume for the digital assets more than doubled in the past year, according to the asset management firm Macquarie Group.

articles.title: Amex soups up cards for Concur
articles.description: The card company and the expense management firm have locked arms to add virtual card features for business clients.

articles.title: Affirm outlines options if economic stress rises
articles.description: The buy now, pay later player spelled out ways in which it could adjust its lending in the event U.S. economic uncertainty creates more stress for consumers.

articles.title: Elavon nabs BofA exec for CEO
articles.description: The payments processor, which is a unit of U.S. Bank, poached Wally Mlynarski from a bank rival to be its CEO, replacing Jamie Walker.
